comment: P7 responded with enthusiasm and validation (e.g., 'Oh nice, I like North Wales', 'that's so cool', 'Yeah, it was really hot', detailed travel stories, and agreement that time went quickly). These are clear signals of engagement, so P4 would infer that P7 enjoyed the interaction more than just polite small talk.

The conversation stayed on comfortable, mildly interesting topics (places lived, travel, weather, hiking). P4 did a lot of the narrative work but P7 reciprocated with questions and shared experiences. There is no sign of boredom or strain, and P4 closes with 'Nice to see you anyway,' suggesting a warm but not deeply excited experience.

Objectively the flow was smooth: P4 asked questions, P7 answered and asked back, and they built on each other's comments about locations and South Africa. There were no long silences or obvious awkwardness, and they even noted that time went quickly. It was a good, warm small-talk-level exchange, though not especially deep or animated.

P4 explicitly contrasted this call with previous problematic ones: 'I think the first video call is actually working properly. The first one had delay...' implying this one worked well. There are no mid-conversation complaints about audio or video, so P4 would rate the call quality as clearly good to excellent.

From P4's perspective, the transcript would look somewhat choppy, with overlapping fragments and some mid-sentence splits, but most of P4's own utterances are intelligible and in the right order. This is slightly better than poor but still below a clean, easy-to-read transcript.

P4 was talkative, volunteered personal background (move from South Africa, family, local area), asked follow-up questions about P7's trip, and commented that the time was up and 'Nice to see you anyway.' These behaviors signal that P4 was engaged and comfortable, so P7 would likely infer that P4 had a good time above a standard baseline.

P7 showed consistent interest: positive reactions ('Oh nice', 'that's so cool'), sharing their own travel experiences, and answering questions in some detail. They did not dominate but participated actively. The interaction seems pleasant and easy, but not especially animated or personally meaningful, fitting a good/warm but not highly excited experience.

The conversation had a clear theme (places, South Africa, weather, hiking), with mutual contributions and no obvious awkward pauses. P7 asked about 'Whereabouts in South Africa were you?' and elaborated on their own trip, indicating a natural back-and-forth. It was more than bare small talk but still light and informational rather than deep, so a solid good-quality exchange.

Although P4 mentioned earlier calls with delay and audio issues, this call itself appears smooth: they can hear each other, respond quickly, and even comment that this one is 'actually working properly.' P7 never complains about the connection, so they would likely rate the videoconference quality as clearly good to excellent.

From P7's side, many utterances are broken into very short fragments and overlap with P4's lines, but the overall content is still recoverable. The transcript is usable but messy, so slightly below average quality rather than completely poor.
